fixture.description: New clone() uses super.clone() and copies a mutable Map field by reference, so clone and original share one backing map; the copy is even flagged mutable. Mutating one silently mutates the other.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that `clone()` performs a shallow copy: `super.clone()` copies the `attributes` reference, so the clone and the original share the same HashMap and a mutation through one is visible in the other (worse, the clone is explicitly made mutable). The fix is to defensively deep-copy the collection (e.g. `copy.attributes = new HashMap<>(attributes)`). Acceptable severity: IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the shared-mutable-collection aliasing is not surfaced. Copying the primitive boolean via super.clone() is fine and should not be the headline.

expected_behavior: DRY/bugs agent flags the shallow clone aliasing the mutable attributes map
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the boolean field copy as the defect
